PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-21-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Malcolm Leader-Picone for Anthony Muschi.

The Court questioned whether Sebastian's two Children that predeceased him had any Children. Mr. Picone stated that James had two Sons, that they were litigants and waived notice, that Frances had no Children and that he believes the judgment and the statement of decision in the other case sets all of this out. Mr. Picone also stated that there are four Grandchildren (Anthony DiFranco, Michael DiFranco, Laura Probert and Allisa DiFranco) and that Anthony, Michael and Laura obtained a judgment against Mr. Muschi. Mr. Picone is to file a declaration establishing the fact that Frances had no Children. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is taken under submission pending receipt of said declaration.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-28-2017 Continued To Sep-11-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Malcolm Leader-Picone for Anthony Muschi.

This was a continued matter. Mr. Leader-Picone requested that the matter be heard today stating that the petition was continued by the Examiner under false pre-tense, that all of his pleadings (publication, notice, consents, etc.) were filed, that courtesy copies were delivered on time, that this petition was filed in 2016, and that he and Ms. Dolch's attorney needed to get paid. The Court explained that courtesy were not received prior to the matter being reviewed and advanced the hearing from September 11, 2017 to September 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Upon further review, Counsel has been notified that the matter is to remain on for hearing on September 11, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 as indicated in the tentative ruling.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (572-580 Chestnut Street, San Francisco, Ca 94133)
Off Calendar Jun-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for petitioner Debra Dolch.

The cause comes on for sale of the real property located at 572-580 Chestnut Street, San Francisco, California, upon the terms and conditions of the Report of Sale filed May 8, 2017.
The opening bid presented was in the sum of $2,759,000.00, which the Court finds is a qualifying bid. There are no overbidders. The sale is confirmed at $2,579.000.00, upon the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property, subject to review of the order. An additional bond in the amount of $1,860,000.00 is required. A status hearing is set for June 20, 2017 at 9:00 am in Department 204 regarding the filing of the required additional bond.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

Off Calendar Feb-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-05-2016 Continued To Sep-11-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The Following matters were heard together: PES-15-298410 Estate of Emilia Di Franco Glessi & PES-16-299708 Estate of Emilia Di Franco Glessi.
Appearances: Malcolm Leader-Picone for Anthony Muschi. Amy Harrington for Laura Probert. Lillian Ng for (special administrator) Debra Dolch. Valerie Horn (via court call) for Laura Probert.

Ms. Ng stated that they are here to confirm the dispute regarding the $180,000.00 ordered in the Judge Bradstreet judgment, that Ms. Horn stated that Mr. Muschi is to pay the $35,200.00, that they are here for instructions and that they filed a supplement regarding the satisfaction of judgment. Ms. Harrington stated that they filed objections on 11/28/16 and went through them, that they are asking for additional interest owed on the interest paid, that the personal judgment against Mr. Muschi should not be paid with estate funds and that the simplest route is that the interest has not been paid. Ms. Horn reported that Ms. Dolch seeks an offset of $35,200.00, that Mr. Muschi has $25,000.00 of it but has spent the other $10,000.00, that Mr. Muschi has waived his claim of offset and is now barred, that he should have raised the claim and defenses in the underlying action, that the issue is whether the $35,200.00 constitutes personal liability or return of property, that they disagree with the special administrator acknowledging a satisfaction of judgment when the attorney's fees and costs are not paid. Mr. Leader-Picone stated that they are not seeking full acknowledgment only partial, that there is a stay by the Court of Appeal, that they agree with Ms. Ng that Judge Bradstreet's statement is clear, that the statement of decision addresses the money issue, that the $180,000.00 is strictly between Mr. Muschi and the estate and that they ask the Court to allow the special administrator to partially acknowledge the judgment. Ms. Ng stated that they have not requested interest on the $180,000.00. Ms. Harrington stated that the beneficiaries have not been determined, that they object to the special administrator waiving interest and that the beneficiaries are entitled to it. Ms. Horn addressed the deposits made by Mr. Muschi, that they are impossible to return to the estate, that they cannot use the offset and that in regards to the interest it accrues as a matter of law and that there is no waiver. The Court stated that regarding the Petition for Instructions it will give the parties until December 12, 2016 to submit their respective proposed orders, agreed to include findings in the order but will not give a tentative ruling now and that the petition will be under submission as of December 12, 2016.

The Court addressed case 298401 and asked what happened when the trial date in June was vacated. Ms. Harrington stated that it was brought back on calendar, that they need a new trial date but that they cannot do anything until the appeal is done. Mr. Leader-Picone confirmed that the appeal stayed the petitions. The Court confirmed that the outstanding petitions are the probate of lost will and the amended petition for probate and that they cannot do anything until after the appeal. The Court continued the petitions to September 11, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the appeal with a written status report due no later than August 28, 2017. Ms. Horn stated that she does not concede that the trust is valid.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-06-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PES-15-298410 Estate of Emilia Di Franco Glessi and PES-16-299708 Estate of Emilia Di Franco Glessi.
Appearances: Malcolm Leader-Picone for and with (petitioner) Anthony Muschi. Amy Harrington for Laura Probert. Lillian Ng for and with Debra Dolch.

The Court stated that we have various pending petitions, that there are issues concerning the will and that the will be probated, confirmed that everyone is in agreement with the appointment of Debra Dolch, stated that she will be appointed as Special Administrator today and that the other matters will be continued for the parties to amend filings. Ms. Harrington stated that the 2006 & 2008 wills need to be set for trial. The Court asked if everyone was ready fro trial and was told no. Ms. Harrington stated that an appeal has been filed in the trust case and they should wait for that. The Court stated that if it makes sense to wait it can continue the matters out and asked Mr. Leader-Picone if he sees the trial going forward before the appeal is finished. Mr. Leader-Picone answered no. The Court stated that they can emerge today with Ms. Dolch being appointed. Ms. Harrington stated they have objections to some of the verbiage on the proposed order. Ms. Ng suggested that she prepare an order. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Special Administration (case 299708)appointing Debra Dolch (subject to receipt/review of the order). The Petition for Probate of will and for letters testamentary (case 299708) was continued to November 28, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard by Judge Cheng, and will also be the date for a possible amended petition that Mr. Leader-Picone might file. The Petition for Letters of Special Administration (case 298410) is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
